personality_voice.book_baseline: In the books, Marchbanks is a senior Wizengamot elder with a sharp, principled voice. Her one direct quote — 'Hogwarts is a school, not an outpost of Cornelius Fudge's office' — is blunt, indignant, and politically courageous. She is also established as socially connected (friend of Augusta Longbottom, known to the Malfoys by reputation) and respected enough that Draco name-drops her and Neville corrects him.
personality_voice.film_portrayal: Marchbanks does not appear in the films at all. The film corpus is empty.
personality_voice.difference: There is no film portrayal to evaluate. Every trait, line, and behavioral signal from the books is absent.
personality_voice.penalty_logic: A score of 0 is the only defensible result when a character has no film presence whatsoever. There is nothing to compare.

narrative_role_agency.book_baseline: Marchbanks serves two narrative functions: (1) she is a high-status dissenter who publicly resigns from the Wizengamot to protest Umbridge's appointment, providing institutional legitimacy to the anti-Ministry position; (2) she is the head of the Wizarding Examinations Authority, making her relevant to the OWL exam arc and a target of Draco's social boasting.
narrative_role_agency.film_portrayal: She does not appear in the films.
narrative_role_agency.difference: Both narrative functions are entirely absent from the films.
narrative_role_agency.penalty_logic: No agency can be evaluated when the character does not exist in the film corpus.

motivations_internal_conflict.book_baseline: Her motivation is clear: principled opposition to Ministry interference in Hogwarts and loyalty to Dumbledore's institutional independence. She acts on conviction, not personal interest.
motivations_internal_conflict.film_portrayal: She does not appear in the films.
motivations_internal_conflict.penalty_logic: No film material exists to evaluate.

character_arc.book_baseline: Marchbanks has a flat arc — she is a principled elder who acts on her values and does not change. Her arc is intentionally static and her function is institutional rather than developmental.
character_arc.film_portrayal: She does not appear in the films.
character_arc.difference: Even a flat arc requires the character to exist in the film.
character_arc.penalty_logic: No film material exists to evaluate.

key_relationships.book_baseline: Two relationships are established: her friendship with Augusta Longbottom (which Neville uses to debunk Draco's boast) and her professional acquaintance with the Malfoy family (which Draco exploits for social currency). Both relationships define her social standing and are used to characterize other characters.
key_relationships.film_portrayal: She does not appear in the films.
key_relationships.penalty_logic: No film material exists to evaluate.

complexity_nuance_lost_material.book_baseline: Marchbanks is a minor but textured character: a powerful institutional figure who acts against her own institution on principle, is socially embedded in both respectable (Longbottom) and morally ambiguous (Malfoy) circles, and is used by the narrative to validate both Neville's social world and the anti-Ministry resistance.
complexity_nuance_lost_material.film_portrayal: She does not appear in the films.
complexity_nuance_lost_material.difference: All nuance and complexity are absent.
complexity_nuance_lost_material.penalty_logic: No film material exists to evaluate.

lost_or_transferred_material: Public resignation from the Wizengamot in protest of Umbridge's appointment — absent from the films
Direct quote condemning Ministry interference in Hogwarts — absent from the films
Role as head of the Wizarding Examinations Authority — absent from the films
Friendship with Augusta Longbottom, used by Neville to correct Draco's boast — absent from the films
Social connection to the Malfoy family used as a characterization beat for Draco — absent from the films
score_caps_applied: No caps formally applied — score is 0 across all dimensions due to complete absence from the films, not due to cap logic
comparative.key_observations: Griselda Marchbanks does not appear in the films at all. Every score is 0 not as a judgment of adaptation quality but as a factual reflection of total absence. The confidence is Low across all dimensions because there is no film material to evaluate — the book corpus establishes a clear, if minor, character, but the film corpus is empty. This is a case of complete omission rather than unfaithful adaptation.
